rows.source_ref: swilliam (opener), "TMT Scalping System" (Trend-Momentum-Trendline), ForexFactory thread 577639, 2018 (indicator zip uploaded Jul 22, 2018); https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/577639/tmt-scalping-system . Original system credited by the opener to BabyPips Free Trading Systems (secondary source, not linked).
rows.concept: Trade a trendline break out of an EMA pullback in the direction of the daily-open bias and EMA(7)/EMA(20) trend, confirmed by RSI and MFI momentum filters.
rows.eligibility_reason: Defining entry trigger (hand-drawn trendline from swing + break) and exits are explicitly discretionary per the thread ('no EA draws the trendlines correctly', 'exit depends on trader'); RSI/MFI filter thresholds are unstated custom-indicator colors, so no deterministic rule set exists as described.
rows.notes: Reconstructable-but-new variant: dropping the trendline and using a mechanical close-back-through-EMA(7) trigger inside a daily-open + EMA(7)>EMA(20) trend, with RSI>50 / MFI>50 as momentum gates and fixed SL 10-20p / RR 1:1-1:3, is a generic FX trend-pullback scalper (Sam68 argues the trendline is secondary and the MA-cross + daily-candle + two momentum 'escalators' are the core) — but that is a substituted, under-specified system, not the one stated. Cost drag severe at 10-20 pip targets on FX. Timeframe variants M1/M5/M15 mentioned; some posters (toppo qqq, Hops) applied it to DAX30/GER30 with wider 35-40 pip SL and traded without SL. Forum source, no track-record evidence beyond screenshot claims. Revisit only if a distinct mechanical breakout definition and indicator thresholds are chosen — treat as a new candidate, not this thread's system.
